title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: file captures complete design context writetfl R package, derived extended design conversation. Read writing code. Companion documents design/: design/DESIGN.md — rationale every decision design/ARCHITECTURE.md — function hierarchy, data flow, internal contracts design/DECISIONS.md — decision log alternatives considered design/TESTING.md — test plan scope Note: docs/ reserved pkgdown site output git-ignored. Keep design/ date. Whenever code change affects following, update relevant file(s) commit PR: Function signatures, names, return values → ARCHITECTURE.md New files, renamed files, removed files → ARCHITECTURE.md Data contracts internal helpers → ARCHITECTURE.md design choice made, choice reversed → DESIGN.md new decision, existing decision changes → DECISIONS.md (add new D-N entry; delete old entries — note change inline) New test files, new test areas, changes testing conventions → TESTING.md

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: writetfl provides exported functions writing ggplot objects, grid grobs, paginated data-frame tables multi-page PDF files precise, composable page layout: export_tfl() — opens PDF device, loops list pages, closes device export_tfl_page() — lays single page header, caption, content, footnote, footer sections using grid viewports tfl_table() — creates paginated table configuration data frame tfl_colspec() — specifies per-column display properties tfl_table() core design goal pixel-precise control whitespace PDF page, specifically supporting clinical/regulatory report aesthetics outer margins, annotation zones, content areas must independently sized never overlap.

title: CLAUDE.md — writetfl package
text: Every page uses two-level viewport stack pushed inside outer_vp: grid.text() / grid.draw() coordinates relative currently active viewport. precise viewport active draw call.
code: PDF page (root viewport, full page)   └── outer_vp         Inset by margins (t/r/b/l) from page edges.         This is the "hard" margin — nothing ever draws outside it.         Coordinates: npc (0,0) = bottom-left of content area.         │         ├── [annotations drawn here while outer_vp is active]         │   header section grobs         │   footer section grobs         │   rule grobs (in padding gaps)         │         └── content_vp               Inset from outer_vp by the space consumed by               header + header_padding + caption + caption_padding (top)               and footnote_padding + footnote + footer_padding + footer (bottom).               The ggplot is printed or grob drawn here.

title: Exporting Figures to PDF
text: Pass one ggplot object directly. page number ("Page 1 1") added bottom-right corner automatically.
code: p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(x = "Weight (1000 lb)", y = "Miles per gallon")  export_tfl(p, preview = TRUE)

title: Exporting Figures to PDF
text: Supply shared annotations via ...; apply every page. report title goes top-left date top-right, separated content full-width rule.
code: plots <- list(   list(content = ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt,  mpg)) + geom_point() +                    labs(title = "Weight vs MPG")),   list(content = ggplot(mtcars, aes(hp,  mpg)) + geom_point() +                    labs(title = "Horsepower vs MPG")),   list(content = ggplot(mtcars, aes(disp, mpg)) + geom_point() +                    labs(title = "Displacement vs MPG")) )  export_tfl(   plots,   file         = "report.pdf",   header_left  = "Fuel Economy Analysis",   header_right = format(Sys.Date(), "%d %b %Y"),   header_rule  = TRUE )

title: Exporting Figures to PDF
text: use ggtibble package generate collection figures, can pass ggtibble object directly export_tfl(). row becomes one page: figure column provides content caption column used automatically. column whose name matches export_tfl_page() text argument (header_left, footnote, etc.) mapped per-page value.  can also add extra annotation columns ggtibble export. Column names must match export_tfl_page() argument names exactly:
code: library(ggtibble) #>  #> Attaching package: 'ggtibble' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:ggplot2': #>  #>     %+%, ggsave  d <- mtcars d$cyl <- factor(d$cyl)  all_plots <-   ggtibble(     d,     ggplot2::aes(x = wt, y = mpg),     outercols = "cyl",     caption   = "Figure: Weight vs MPG for {cyl}-cylinder vehicles"   ) +   geom_point()  # Preview all pages (one per cylinder group) export_tfl(   all_plots,   preview      = TRUE,   header_left  = "Fuel Economy by Cylinder Count",   header_right = format(Sys.Date(), "%d %b %Y"),   header_rule  = TRUE ) all_plots$footnote <- paste("n =", table(d$cyl)[as.character(all_plots$cyl)])  export_tfl(all_plots, file = "by_cylinder.pdf",            header_left = "Report Title",            header_rule = TRUE)

title: Exporting Figures to PDF
text: grid grob accepted content, figures hand-assembled grobs can mixed one multi-page PDF. example uses gridExtra::tableGrob() drop plain matrix table onto page alongside figure. richly paginated data-frame tables — automatic column widths, word-wrapping, row column pagination, group-aware page breaks — use tfl_table() instead. See vignette("v02-tfl_table_intro") details.
code: # Any grid grob works as content library(gridExtra) tbl_grob <- tableGrob(head(mtcars[, 1:5], 10))  export_tfl(   list(     list(       content  = tbl_grob,       caption  = "Table 1. First 10 rows of the mtcars dataset.",       footnote = "Source: Motor Trend (1974)."     ),     list(       content = ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point(),       caption = "Figure 1. Weight vs MPG."     )   ),   file        = "mixed.pdf",   header_left = "Analysis Report",   header_rule = TRUE )

title: Exporting Figures to PDF
text: left right header footer text wide enough risk collision, writetfl warns errors automatically. Gap < 0 (true overlap) → error drawing. 0 ≤ gap < overlap_warn_mm mm → rlang::warn() (drawing still proceeds). overlap_warn_mm = NULL → detection disabled entirely.
code: # Tighten the warning threshold to catch moderate crowding early export_tfl(   p,   file            = "overlap-check.pdf",   header_left     = "A moderately long left header",   header_right    = "A moderately long right header",   overlap_warn_mm = 20 )  # Silence overlap detection for a layout you have manually verified export_tfl(   p,   file            = "no-overlap-check.pdf",   header_left     = "Left",   header_right    = "Right",   overlap_warn_mm = NULL )

title: Creating Tables with tfl_table
text: Pass data frame tfl_table(), hand result export_tfl(). Page annotations, page dimensions, output file belong export_tfl() — tfl_table().  tfl_table() returns table configuration object. draw anything open device. drawing happens inside export_tfl().
code: tbl <- tfl_table(head(mtcars, 20))  export_tfl(   tbl,   preview     = TRUE,   header_left = "Table 1. Motor Trend Car Road Tests (first 20 rows)",   footer_left = "Source: Motor Trend (1974)",   header_rule = TRUE )

title: Creating Tables with tfl_table
text: default, column names used column headers. Supply col_labels override — either named character vector (match column name) positional vector length cols.  Embedding \n label creates multi-line column header. header row height sized automatically fit tallest label.
code: # Subset columns and rename them for the report tbl <- tfl_table(   head(mtcars, 20)[, c("mpg", "cyl", "hp", "wt")],   col_labels = c(     mpg = "Miles/Gallon",     cyl = "Cylinders",     hp  = "Horsepower",     wt  = "Weight\n(1000 lb)"   # \n produces a two-line header   ) )  export_tfl(   tbl,   preview     = TRUE,   header_left = "Table 1. Selected Performance Metrics" )

title: Creating Tables with tfl_table
text: Three width modes available can mixed freely within table. relative widths used, scaled proportionally among fixed auto columns claimed space.
code: tbl <- tfl_table(   head(mtcars, 20)[, c("mpg", "cyl", "hp", "wt", "gear")],   col_widths = list(     mpg  = unit(1.2, "inches"),   # fixed     cyl  = 1,                     # relative (equal share of remaining space)     hp   = 1,                     # relative (equal share)     wt   = unit(1.4, "inches"),   # fixed     gear = NULL                   # auto: sized to content   ) )  export_tfl(tbl, file = "column_widths.pdf")

title: Creating Tables with tfl_table
text: Numeric columns default right-aligned; character columns default left-aligned. Override per column col_align.  Valid alignment values "left", "right", "center".
code: ae_summary <- data.frame(   system_organ_class = c("Gastrointestinal", "Nervous system", "Skin"),   n_subjects         = c(12L, 7L, 4L),   pct                = c(24.0, 14.0, 8.0),   stringsAsFactors   = FALSE )  tbl <- tfl_table(   ae_summary,   col_labels = c(     system_organ_class = "System Organ Class",     n_subjects         = "n",     pct                = "(%)"   ),   col_align = c(     system_organ_class = "left",     n_subjects         = "right",     pct                = "right"   ) )  export_tfl(   tbl,   preview     = TRUE,   header_left = "Table 2. Adverse Events by System Organ Class",   footnote    = "Percentages are based on the safety population (N = 50)." )

title: Creating Tables with tfl_table
text: Mark grouping columns dplyr::group_by() passing data tfl_table(). Group columns must appear first column list; act row headers values suppressed repeated consecutive rows, giving indented-group appearance common clinical tables.  suppress_repeated_groups argument (default TRUE) controls whether repeated group values hidden. Set FALSE show every row’s group value explicitly. Group values within block kept together page wherever possible (see Multi-page tables ).
code: # Demographic summary with visit and treatment group as row headers pk_data <- data.frame(   visit     = rep(c("Week 4", "Week 8", "Week 12"), each = 4),   treatment = rep(c("Placebo", "Active 10 mg", "Active 20 mg", "Active 40 mg"), 3),   n         = c(48L, 50L, 49L, 51L, 45L, 47L, 48L, 50L, 41L, 43L, 44L, 46L),   mean_auc  = c(120.4, 145.2, 178.9, 201.3,                 118.7, 148.6, 185.2, 219.4,                 115.1, 152.3, 191.7, 228.6),   sd_auc    = c(18.2, 22.4, 27.6, 31.1,                 17.9, 23.1, 28.4, 32.7,                 17.1, 24.0, 29.2, 34.3),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  tbl <- pk_data |>   group_by(visit) |>   tfl_table(     col_labels = c(       visit     = "Visit",       treatment = "Treatment",       n         = "n",       mean_auc  = "Mean AUC\n(ng\u00b7h/mL)",       sd_auc    = "SD"     ),     col_widths = list(       visit     = unit(1.0, "inches"),       treatment = unit(1.5, "inches"),       n         = NULL,       mean_auc  = unit(1.4, "inches"),       sd_auc    = unit(0.8, "inches")     )   )  export_tfl(   tbl,   preview     = TRUE,   header_left = "Table 3. PK Summary by Visit and Treatment",   footnote    = "AUC = area under the concentration-time curve." )

title: Creating Tables with tfl_table
text: table rows fit one page, tfl_table paginates automatically. Groups kept together: rows belonging one group value fit current page, whole group moves next page. Continuation markers appended last column header intermediate pages first data row continuation page reader can follow table across page breaks.
code:
tbl <- iris |>   relocate(Species) |>   group_by(Species) |>   tfl_table(     col_labels = c(       Species      = "Species",       Sepal.Length = "Sepal
Length",       Sepal.Width  = "Sepal
Width",       Petal.Length = "Petal
Length",       Petal.Width  = "Petal
Width"     ),     row_cont_msg = c("(continued from previous page)", "(continued on next page)"),     col_cont_msg = "(continued)"   )  export_tfl(   tbl,   preview     = c(1, 2),   header_left = "Table 4. Iris Measurements by Species",   header_rule = TRUE,   footer_rule = TRUE ) #> Warning: Row 33 belongs to a group that spans more than one page. title: Styling Tables with tfl_table
text: Row-header (group) columns — designated via dplyr::group_by() — receive style key, gp$group_col, also inherits gp$table.  override single group column without touching others, pass gp directly tfl_colspec():  gp tfl_colspec() takes precedence gp$group_col specific column; group columns still inherit gp$group_col.
code: # Bold group column to distinguish it from data columns tbl <- clinical |>   group_by(treatment) |>   tfl_table(     cols = list(       tfl_colspec("treatment",  label = "Treatment Arm", width = unit(1.3, "inches")),       tfl_colspec("subgroup",   label = "Subgroup",      width = unit(1.4, "inches")),       tfl_colspec("n",          label = "N",             width = unit(0.4, "inches")),       tfl_colspec("rate_pct",   label = "Rate (%)",      width = unit(0.65, "inches"))     ),     gp = list(       group_col = gpar(fontface = "bold")     )   )  export_tfl(tbl, preview = TRUE, header_left = "Group column: bold") # The treatment group column gets bold via its tfl_colspec gp; # any other group columns would stay at the gp$group_col default tbl <- clinical |>   group_by(treatment) |>   tfl_table(     cols = list(       tfl_colspec("treatment",  label = "Treatment Arm", width = unit(1.3, "inches"),                   gp = gpar(fontface = "bold")),       tfl_colspec("subgroup",   label = "Subgroup",      width = unit(1.4, "inches")),       tfl_colspec("n",          label = "N",             width = unit(0.4, "inches")),       tfl_colspec("rate_pct",   label = "Rate (%)",      width = unit(0.65, "inches"))     )   )  export_tfl(tbl, preview = TRUE,            header_left = "Per-colspec gp overrides group_col gp")

what: 6. Continuation marker style — gp$continued and row_cont_msg
title: Styling Tables with tfl_table
text: table spans multiple pages, tfl_table() injects continuation marker bottom non-final page. default marker text "(continued)" rendered italic. row_cont_msg replaces default "(continued)" string. gp$continued controls visual rendering whatever text row_cont_msg provides. continuation marker appears tables rows fit one page; see vignette("v02-tfl_table_intro") example.

text: cell_padding grid::unit object controls whitespace cell content cell boundaries. accepts two forms: Scalar — padding applied four sides:  Two-element vector — separate vertical horizontal padding. title: Troubleshooting writetfl
text: Error: Content height (X ) minimum (Y ). error means reserving space headers, footers, captions, footnotes, isn’t enough room left main content area. Common causes: short page height (pg_height) Large margins Lengthy captions footnotes consume vertical space Multiple annotation sections combined Solutions: Increase pg_height decrease margins Shorten caption/footnote text Reduce annotation font sizes via gp argument Lower min_content_height (default 3 inches) content genuinely needs less space:
code: export_tfl(x, file = "out.pdf",            min_content_height = grid::unit(2, "inches"))

title: Troubleshooting writetfl
text: Error: Total column width (X ) exceeds available content width (Y ) wrapping. error appears table columns wide fit single page allow_col_split = FALSE. error message includes per-column width breakdown can identify columns consuming space. Solutions: Enable column splitting: tfl_table(..., allow_col_split = TRUE) Enable word wrapping wide columns: tfl_table(..., wrap_cols = c("wide_col1", "wide_col2")) Set narrower fixed widths specific columns via tfl_colspec() Increase page width decrease margins

title: Troubleshooting writetfl
text: Warning: Header/footer elements may overlap warning fires combined width left, center, right elements header footer row close exceeds available width. Solutions: Shorten header/footer text Use smaller font sizes sections via gp Remove center element left + right sufficient Suppress warning overlap_warn_mm = NULL ’ve verified layout acceptable

title: Getting Started with writetfl
text: tfl_table() converts data frame paginated table grob. Pass result directly export_tfl().  Use dplyr::group_by() designate row-header columns. Group columns repeat every column-split page suppress repeated values consecutive rows.  tfl_table() paginates automatically: Row pagination — rows split across pages optional (continued) markers; groups kept together possible. Column pagination — columns exceed page width split across pages; set balance_col_pages = TRUE distribute columns evenly. Column widths — auto-sized content, fixed (unit()), relative-weight numeric. Word wrapping — wrap_cols reflows long text within fixed column width. complete table reference — column specs, continuation messages, cell padding, line height, — see vignette("v02-tfl_table_intro"). table typography styling, see vignette("v03-tfl_table_styling").

text: tfl_table() builds table configuration object export_tfl() paginates automatically across many pages needed: Column widths — auto-sized content, fixed (unit()), relative-weight numeric. floor applied via min_col_width. Word wrapping — set wrap_cols column name (TRUE data columns) reflow long text within fixed column width. Row pagination — rows split across pages optional continuation markers (row_cont_msg). Groups kept together possible; warning issued group must split. Column pagination — total column width exceeds page, columns split across pages. Set balance_col_pages = TRUE distribute columns evenly rather packing left--right. Group columns — use dplyr::group_by() passing tfl_table(). Group columns repeat row headers every column-split page; repeated values consecutive rows suppressed default. Typography spacing — cell_padding controls space inside cell (vertical horizontal independently); line_height controls inter-line spacing wrapped cells. can overridden per section via gp. Column specs — use tfl_colspec() per-column control label, width, alignment, wrapping single object.

text: Renders single page five vertical sections: header, caption, content, footnote, footer. Section heights computed dynamically font metrics content area occupies remaining space. layout errors (overlapping elements, content area short) collected reported together drawing occurs.

title: Lay out and render a single TFL page — export_tfl_page
code: export_tfl_page(   x,   padding = grid::unit(0.5, "lines"),   header_left = NULL,   header_center = NULL,   header_right = NULL,   caption = NULL,   footnote = NULL,   footer_left = NULL,   footer_center = NULL,   footer_right = NULL,   gp = grid::gpar(),   header_rule = FALSE,   footer_rule = FALSE,   caption_just = "left",   footnote_just = "left",   margins = grid::unit(c(t = 0.5, r = 0.5, b = 0.5, l = 0.5), "inches"),   min_content_height = grid::unit(3, "inches"),   page_i = NULL,   preview = FALSE,   ... )

title: Lay out and render a single TFL page — export_tfl_page
text: x list required content element (ggplot object grid grob, e.g. gt::as_gtable() gridExtra::tableGrob()) optional text elements: header_left, header_center, header_right, caption, footnote, footer_left, footer_center, footer_right. List elements take precedence corresponding direct arguments. padding Vertical space adjacent present sections, unit object. Separator rules (enabled) drawn midpoint gap consume additional space. header_left, header_center, header_right Header text. Accepts NULL, single string, character vector (collapsed "\\n"). Horizontal justification follows argument name (left/center/right). Vertically top-justified. Overridden x$header_left etc. caption Caption text header content. Accepts NULL, single string, character vector. Full-width; justification controlled caption_just. Overridden x$caption. footnote Footnote text content. Accepts NULL, single string, character vector. Full-width; justification controlled footnote_just. Overridden x$footnote. footer_left, footer_center, footer_right Footer text. Mirror header arguments. Vertically bottom-justified. Overridden x$footer_left etc. gp Typography specification. Accepts either single gpar() object applied text, named list section- element-level control. Resolution priority (highest first): element-level (e.g. gp$header_left), section-level (e.g. gp$header), global gpar(). Example:   header_rule Separator rule drawn header next section (caption content), fitted within padding gap. Accepts: FALSE: rule TRUE: full-width rule numeric (0, 1]: rule spanning fraction viewport width, centered grob (typically linesGrob): drawn -, centered vertically padding gap. footer_rule Separator rule last body section (footnote content) footer. specification header_rule. caption_just Horizontal justification caption. footnote_just Horizontal justification footnote. margins Outer page margins unit vector elements t, r, b, l (top, right, bottom, left). min_content_height Minimum acceptable content area height unit object. error raised computed content height falls value. page_i Integer page index, used prefix layout error messages "Page <>: ". Set automatically export_tfl(); normally supplied calling function directly. preview Logical. TRUE, calls grid.newpage() draws currently open device without opening closing device. ... Additional arguments. Currently recognised: overlap_warn_mm: numeric threshold mm near-miss overlap warnings. Set NULL disable.
code: gp = list(   header        = gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "bold"),   header_right  = gpar(fontsize =  9, col = "gray50"),   caption       = gpar(fontsize =  9, fontface = "italic"),   footer        = gpar(fontsize =  8) )

what: Define a data frame table for PDF export — tfl_table
title: Define a data frame table for PDF export — tfl_table
text: Creates table configuration object can passed export_tfl(). measurement, pagination, rendering deferred export_tfl() called, point page layout information (dimensions, margins, annotations) available. Column properties can specified via tfl_colspec() objects cols, via flat arguments (col_widths, col_labels, etc.), . name column, tfl_colspec() takes priority. Row-header columns (repeat every column-split page) detected automatically dplyr::group_vars(x). Group columns must appear first columns x order dplyr::group_vars(x); error raised .

title: Define a data frame table for PDF export — tfl_table
code: tfl_table(   x,   cols = NULL,   col_widths = NULL,   col_labels = NULL,   col_align = NULL,   wrap_cols = FALSE,   min_col_width = grid::unit(0.5, "inches"),   allow_col_split = TRUE,   balance_col_pages = FALSE,   suppress_repeated_groups = TRUE,   col_cont_msg = "Columns continue on other pages",   row_cont_msg = c("(continued)", "(continued on next page)"),   show_col_names = TRUE,   col_header_rule = TRUE,   group_rule = TRUE,   group_rule_after_last = FALSE,   row_rule = FALSE,   row_header_sep = FALSE,   fill_by = "row",   na_string = "",   gp = list(),   cell_padding = grid::unit(c(0.2, 0.5), "lines"),   line_height = 1.05,   max_measure_rows = Inf )

title: Define a data frame table for PDF export — tfl_table
text: x data frame grouped tibble (dplyr::group_by()). cols list tfl_colspec() objects, NULL auto-specify columns. cols provided may partial — columns named cols fall back flat arguments type-based defaults. Column display order always follows source data frame, regardless order entries cols. col_widths Named vector: element either unit object (fixed width) plain positive numeric (relative weight). Names must match column names x. Overridden per-column tfl_colspec(width). col_labels Named character vector display labels. Use "\n" multiline column headers. Overridden per-column tfl_colspec(label). col_align Named character vector. element "left", "right", "centre". Overridden per-column tfl_colspec(align). wrap_cols Column-wrapping eligibility. TRUE = non-group columns eligible; FALSE = none eligible; character vector = specific column names. Overridden per-column tfl_colspec(wrap). min_col_width Minimum column width unit object. allow_col_split Logical. FALSE, error raised total column width still exceeds available width wrapping. TRUE (default), columns split across pages. balance_col_pages Logical. TRUE column pagination produces one page, data columns redistributed across pages page receives approximately number columns. greedy pass still used determine minimum number pages required, balanced group verified fit; balanced group overflow available width greedy layout used fallback. Default FALSE. suppress_repeated_groups Logical. TRUE (default), group column cells whose value equals immediately preceding rendered row page left blank. first data row page always shows group value. col_cont_msg Character scalar NULL. Message displayed rotated side labels column-split pages: clockwise 90° right table columns continue subsequent page, counter-clockwise 90° left table (including row-label columns) columns continue prior page. Set NULL disable. row_cont_msg Character vector length 1 2. first element shown top continuation page; second shown bottom preceding page. length-1 value recycled positions. Default: c("(continued)", "(continued next page)"). show_col_names Logical. FALSE, column header row omitted col_header_rule also suppressed. col_header_rule Logical. TRUE (default), horizontal rule drawn column header row. group_rule Logical. TRUE (default), horizontal rule drawn row groups. group_rule_after_last Logical. TRUE, rule also drawn last group page. Default FALSE. row_rule Logical. TRUE, horizontal rule drawn every data row. Style controlled via gp$row_rule. Default FALSE. row_header_sep Logical. TRUE, vertical rule drawn right edge last row-header column, spanning data rows (column header row). Default FALSE. fill_by Character scalar controlling gp$data_row$fill color vectors cycled. "row" (default) advances color index every data row. "group" advances group boundaries, rows group share one fill color. na_string Character scalar. Replacement text NA values. Default "". gp named list gpar() objects controlling table-internal typography rule styles. Page-annotation typography controlled separately via gp argument export_tfl_page(). Recognised keys: gp$table Base font table text. gp$header_row Column header row. Default: bold. Set fill background color (e.g., gpar(fontface = "bold", fill = "lightblue")). gp$data_row Data cell text. Inherits gp$table. Set fill background color; use vector alternating rows groups (e.g., gpar(fill = c("white", "gray95"))). See fill_by. gp$group_col Row-header column cells. Inherits gp$table. gp$continued Continuation-marker row text. Default: italic. gp$col_header_rule Style column-header rule. gp$group_rule Style -group rules. gp$row_rule Style -row data rules. gp$row_header_sep Style vertical row-header separator. cell_padding Padding inside cell. Accepts unit length: 1: applied four sides 2: c(vertical, horizontal) — first element top/bottom, second left/right 4: c(top, right, bottom, left) — CSS-style per-side control Example: unit(c(0.2, 0.5), "lines") 0.2 lines vertical, 0.5 lines horizontal. Note: named vectors supported grid::unit() preserve names numeric vectors. line_height positive numeric multiplier controls spacing lines within multi-line (word-wrapped) cell. value 1.0 packs lines baseline--baseline extra gap; default 1.05 adds small 5% breathing room. gpar() supplied gp argument already contains explicit lineheight field particular section, value takes precedence parameter. max_measure_rows Positive numeric Inf (default). Maximum number unique cell strings sampled per column computing content-based column widths. Strings sampled descending order nchar() widest strings always measured. Also limits number data rows sampled row-height estimation.
